articles.title: How coach-on-pitch Messi undid England's tactics on the fly

articles.title: Scotland keeper Gordon retires
articles.summary: Goalkeeper Craig Gordon announces his retirement from football, bringing an end to a 25-year playing career.

articles.title: Midfielder Gomes close to £38m Villa move
articles.summary: Joao Gomes is set to leave Wolves after their relegation from the Premier League and join Aston Villa.

articles.title: Goalkeeper Muller joins Aberdeen on two-year deal
articles.summary: Goalkeeper Marius Muller hopes to "have plenty to celebrate" after joining Aberdeen from Wolfsburg.

articles.title: Record crowd as Kerr returns to Gotham FC
articles.summary: Former Chelsea forward Sam Kerr makes her second debut for Gotham FC in front of the largest ever crowd for a women's sporting event in New York City.

articles.summary: England are out. Rick and Lloyd react.

articles.title: Goalkeeper Gordon, 43, retires after 25-year career
articles.summary: Craig Gordon has retired from playing football at the age of 43.

articles.title: How hard is it for Littler to go back to back at the World Matchplay?
articles.summary: The World Matchplay was a tournament dominated by Phil Taylor as - from 1994 to 2014 - he picked up 15 wins in Blackpool out of a possible 21. Yes, you read that correctly.

articles.title: Hibs to face Kosovan side Malisheva in Europe
articles.summary: Hibernian will face Malisheva in the second round of Conference League qualifying later this month after the Kosovan outfit beat Albanian side Vllaznia 6-2 on aggregate.

articles.title: Cardiff to appeal against dismissed £100m Sala claim
articles.summary: Cardiff City will appeal against a French judge's decision to throw out the club's claim for more than £100m in compensation following the death of footballer Emiliano Sala.

articles.title: Morecambe first club to face action from regulator
articles.summary: The independent football regulator issues a warning to Morecambe after their owners fail to respond to requests for information.

articles.summary: Ireland have brought back their big names for the crunch Nations Championship clash against the All Blacks at Eden Park on Saturday.

articles.title: Tuchel retains FA backing despite England defeat
articles.summary: Thomas Tuchel retains the backing of the Football Association despite England failing to reach the World Cup final.

articles.title: Argentina shatter England's World Cup dreams in semi-final
articles.summary: Argentina shatter England's World Cup dreams, scoring two late goals to come from behind and knock out Thomas Tuchel's side, in a dramatic semi-final in Atlanta.

articles.title: 'No regrets' - Tuchel on England's defeat by Argentina
articles.summary: England manager Thomas Tuchel believes his team "got too passive" after scoring first in the World Cup semi-final against Argentina, which they lost 2-1 at Atlanta Stadium.

articles.title: Final two World Cup matches to be shown live on BBC
articles.summary: Saturday's World Cup third-place play-off between England and France and Sunday's World Cup final will both be  live on BBC TV.

articles.title: All the goals from England's 2026 World Cup campaign so far
articles.summary: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there.

articles.title: All of Argentina's goals from the World Cup so far
articles.summary: Watch all 17 of Argentina's goals from World Cup 2026 on their way to the semi-final, where they face England.

articles.title: Headers caused Stiles' brain disease, coroner finds
articles.summary: England 1966 World Cup winner Nobby Stiles died with a condition caused by heading footballs, his inquest concludes.

articles.title: World Cup-winner Stiles' death linked to heading footballs, coroner rules
articles.summary: England 1966 World Cup winner Nobby Stiles died with a brain condition caused by repeatedly heading a football, a coroner has ruled.

articles.title: Spain into World Cup final with victory over France
articles.summary: Spain are into the 2026 World Cup final after goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro see them convincingly beat France 2-0 in their semi-final in Dallas.
